item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rating +128 comments 121 (before mine). I know much of this is from downvotes on earlier versions but I'm glad it didn't get deleted with the rewrite freshly posted. Got to enjoy the discussion!

Also one of that 128 is my +1 cause LASER SHARKS HELL YEAH

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No one mentioned the fact they are literally cyclops sharks in both the medical and the "X-Men" meaning of the word?
I laughed. A lot.
+1

item.title: Re: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Pozlp_wnkRk
item.content:encoded: Stole my comment years before I even thought to make it, but I love you for it. Yeah Baby.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THEY HAVE A THEMESONG NOW 8D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pet peeve (and not something that has any impact on my voting for the article): "cyclopic holoprosencephaly" isn't an actual thing. Well, it is a thing, but that's not the term used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My guess? Totally not finished products. Possibly, they'll never be finished and -A and -B were let loose into the ocean in some misguided attempt to "Let them go FREE!"

Seriously, that is precisely the sort of mistake Isabel would make upon seeing her dad's horrifically malformed, half-baked idea sitting in an aquarium somewhere. I can't see her having the gumption to do the "right" thing in that situation, except maybe after the arc Djoric had planned for AIC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think Wondertainment would make something so obviously DANGEROUS. They're basically guns. That don't work right come to that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I fell for the shmuck bait. I can personally attest that Google image search for "cyclopic holoprosencephaly" results in mostly malformed stillborn/miscarried human babies.

It ain't pretty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you meant the line about "As is typical with organisms afflicted with holoprosencephaly, the majority of the brain is absent", that's because cyclopic holoprosencephaly is a real thing. WARNING: Google will show you many pictures of horribly deformed stillborn/miscarried babies of multiple species, possibly including humans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I ended up loving it but this should have been made a -J after the rewrite.
It's no small feat to get a +1 from me for using the term 'laser cells' on a mainlister. Also really like the implication that the Foundation has had to deal with other laser-animals before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1373's image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact weizhong or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The shark punching centre got a lot of work on their hands

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too silly for me. A shark with a laser gun sticking out of its head is goofy, especially if the gun looks like one of those "futuristic" rayguns from the 1950's. As these things are now, they feel like a bad science experiment that failed. Which, granted, could be the point. If they had better control over their beams it might seem less oddball.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *FOREHEADS.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All I want is Sharks with FRIKIN' LAZER BEAMS ATTACHED TO THEIR HEADS. upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this was going to be a stupid idea, but I actually was kind cool with the presentation and all. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It got better when the staff somehow presented pig #3 to the unwitting pranksters the very next day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Were your friends named Lemonjello and Orangejello? Or maybe they were making out in the car before they picked up the pigs, and they heard scratching on the car, then later found a hook hand stuck to the door handle?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dont ask why, but this kinda reminds me of a prank my friends pulled a couple years ago in their high school. They let three pigs loose in the school numbered 1,2, and 4. The staff spent the next couple of days frantically searching for the pig 3.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now we need monkeys flinging radioactive poop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I confess I was on the fence about this, becuase on one hand the internal logic and analysis of "what would this actually look\work like" is well executed. On the other hand, I had a hard time taking this seriously.

So, I read the discussion page. and realized these sharks might be someone with the means and will to make cheesy b-movie "monsters" a reality.

And that is terrifying.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could say that this was too hard to take seriously….except that this is wondertainment. This is exactly the sort of thing they both could and would create. The facepalm reaction to it here combined with its the potential hazard it represents shows me that it has its desired effect and is functioning properly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the revision much better, thought i liked it more when the text that epxlained how the lasers work was more hard-fi

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This revision earned my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On further research it is listed as a verb in the dictionary, still seems like an non-clinical term though. Despite that it is likely used in laser surgery clinics a lot.

"Received lethal wounds by laser" feels more cromulent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The first batch is sad. The second batch is not.

And what verb would you recommend?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's more tragic now but it is still laser sharks.

Also

three crew members were lasered to death

stop verbing perfectly good nouns, mister!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do not like this laser shark
I would not bring it on an ark
I do not want it on my boat
I would not cross it with a goat
Please no more with sharks and bees
These silly memes make my brain freeze

Dr. Suess downvotes for me…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude, there are times where peppermint is a *bad* smell. Just thought I'd give you a heads up.

item.title: Re: STAFF POST!
item.content:encoded: Voting for deletion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought he just liked the way I smelled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you write that, I will read it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd recommend deleting so that the rewrite gets a fresh start on the vote tally.

item.title: Re: STAFF POST!
item.content:encoded:
Third for Bright.

Voct wants to rewrite, reversing vote for him.

item.title: Re: STAFF POST!
item.content:encoded: You have my vote!

item.title: Re: STAFF POST!
item.content:encoded: Also voting against, for the same reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
okay, here's an idea for a rewrite.

There are at least two batches of these things.

The first batch had holoprosencephaly such that they were practically in a vegetative state. They barely move, and once you use them for Happy Laser Fun Time, they shudder and die spasmodically (with lasers spurting everywhere).

The second batch…

well, we don't quite know. Because they escaped, you see. Into the wild.

And now there's ships and submarines reporting unusual hull damage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to say I like this one. It does have the minor side effect of wanting one of them though.

Say, what happens if we get some that have come to term, and the baby sharks start pulling on each other. Even more fun if the shark embryos start pulling at each other in the mother's womb…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know it's a real shark, but damned if ti doesn't look like the alien from lilo and stitch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upon request, my reasons for downvoting.

(5:06:29 PM) Rights: It's one of those articles where I read it, and I go "…Not sure if joke article or just not that good"
(5:07:14 PM) Rights: It's well written, yeah, and I get the whole Wondertainment thing (though I haven't been around much so it's like 'where the hell did these guys come from') but it doesn't sit well with me, as an SCP
(5:07:38 PM) Rights: Like I said
(5:08:10 PM) Rights: My brain can't decide if it's supposed to be a joke or serious. It's not particularly wonder, thrill, or fear inspiring in me, and if it doesn't meet at least one of those…
(5:08:16 PM) Voct: you do know that the picture of a shark fetus with cyclopic holoprosencephaly is real, yes?
(5:08:20 PM) Rights: But that's my opinion. I didn't enjoy it /personally/
(5:08:21 PM) Rights: Yes, I know
(5:08:30 PM) Rights: Believe it or not, the biology nerd knows the whole story behind it
(5:08:46 PM) Rights: :I
(5:09:20 PM) Rights: THe picture's a genetic anomoly, but it's more impressive to me IRL than it is as a supernatural effect.

item.title: Re: STAFF POST!
item.content:encoded: Nnnn… voting against deletion, despite my own downvote. Too many folks like this idea so I think we should give it a chance to be improved before letting it go.

item.title: Re: STAFF POST!
item.content:encoded: Seconding!~

item.title: STAFF POST!
item.content:encoded:
Advocating Deletion at -5.

A reminder, only staff are allowed to comment in staff only posts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: not if the SCP itself gets deleted I'm not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Voct, you are now obliged to write that Tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excuse me while I go take out a restraining order.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And this, kids, is how you respond to criticism. :)

(Remember, it's only a fuck-up if you don't learn from it. If you learn from it, it's called experience.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: :) I read it when joining, but had forgotten there was a list. I have reread it now (someone linked to it earlier in the thread). Personally, I have no problem looking like a dumbass so long as I learn from the experience, which I feel I have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 Best Speculative Post About Wondertainment Shark Products

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's already a Wondertainment product involving hamsters and semen.

And it works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
unfortunately, the jet-propelled ones tore their way out of the mother before they were ready.

Imagine a shark, swimming peacefully along, happy in the knowledge of new life in her womb, when suddenly she feels a twitch deep inside her and BOOM all of her babies BLAST through her flesh, tearing her to pieces

and the last thing her shark brain thinks before she dies horribly is "but they're not ready yet?"

and the jet-propelled shark fetuses die horribly because seawater isn't a suitable replacement for amniotic fluid, especially not when you're a fetus with cyclopic holoprosencephaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is exactly why I had to upvote this. Hell, if I were Dr. Wondertainment, this is what I would have done, except that they'd also be jet-propelled.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you had read the guide material you are supposed to have read as a condition of joining the site, fuzzyboots, you'd look less like a dumbass right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You assume I don't already do this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heck, the pop-culture reference could even be why it exists (although of course the Foundation would never be able to know that). Perhaps Dr. Wondertainment, who/whatever he/she/they is/are, was/were watching Austin Powers one day, and thought to him/her/themself/ves, "Frickin' sharks with frickin' laser beams in their foreheads? Why not??"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So now you'll start stalking senior staff members to be near?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Scantron

Nowhere near senior staff

Challenge accepted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No. I can only speak for myself, but don't think I ever felt compelled to vote in line with senior staff, or anything. Besides, look at the + and - vote distribution at a bunch of articles yourself - I guarantee what you will see is that unless something is very good, or very bad, the votes will be split.
The reason why it might appear so is, that the senior staff have in general, a very good idea in what makes an article work (in fact, that's the main reason for promotions around here, AFAIK) , and have seen a lot of them, which is also a bonus - a lot of ideas have been independently come to by different people.
Furthermore, which is probably a side-effect of most of them writing well, they can state their opinions quite well - often what might seem like "herding" is just someone senior expressing the unstated opinion of dozens of people better than they could do it themselves (thus leaving them little need to repeat what's been said.)

Lastly, the senior staff list is in the Guide to Newbies - anyone not there just ain't Staff. There are a lot of old users, and prolific users, and prolific old users who are nowhere near it, even though they tend to be vocal about happenings around here (Scantron and Mackenzie are good examples). They have often valuable insight, but they are by no means staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
{nods} There is that. And my apologies if I confused the Staff divide. New here. :) They're people with high karma who have a personnel page, so that's best I could tell.

Admittedly, being dead might be a bit of a barrier to Dr. Magnus, albeit not an insurmountable one given where he works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The day Magnus becomes staff is the day I stage a bloody coup and take over this wiki, because something is obviously terribly, terribly wrong.

Also, while staff votes rarely make a difference, I do think that sometimes staff posts can tip the balance. Generally because we make people notice why an article isn't working.
